examples.title: Algorithm: Elliptic Curve Point Addition
examples.description: Demonstrates the geometric operation of point addition on an elliptic curve
examples.code:
FUNCTION addPoints(P, Q)
 IF P = O THEN RETURN Q
 IF Q = O THEN RETURN P
 IF P.x = Q.x AND P.y = -Q.y THEN RETURN O
 m = (Q.y - P.y) / (Q.x - P.x)
 R.x = m^2 - P.x - Q.x
 R.y = m * (P.x - R.x) - P.y
 RETURN R
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the geometric operation of point addition on an elliptic curve. It takes two points P and Q as input and returns their sum R.

examples.title: Python: ECDSA Signature Generation
examples.description: Demonstrates the process of generating a digital signature using ECDSA
examples.code:
import hashlib
import random

def generate_signature(private_key, message):
 # Select a random integer k
 k = random.randint(1, private_key.curve.order - 1)
 # Compute (x1, y1) = k * G
 x1, y1 = k * private_key.curve.generator
 # Compute r = x1 mod n
 r = x1 % private_key.curve.order
 # Compute s = k^-1 * (H(m) + d * r) mod n
 s = pow(k, -1, private_key.curve.order) * (hashlib.sha256(message.encode()).hexdigest() + private_key.d * r) % private_key.curve.order
 return r, s
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates the process of generating a digital signature using ECDSA. It takes a private key and a message as input and returns the signature (r, s).

examples.title: C: ECDH Key Exchange
examples.description: Demonstrates the process of establishing a shared secret using ECDH
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates the process of establishing a shared secret using ECDH. It defines the elliptic curve parameters and computes the shared secret using the private key and public key.
